title: Chung

jokes.text: Because it's better than watching Gary drip that fucking noodle juice down his chin.
jokes.setup: Why would the VP attend Chung's book launch
jokes.punchline: The alternative is watching Gary's disgusting eating habits
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Selina's petty disdain and Gary's pathetic nature. Visual imagery is gross but memorable.

jokes.text: God, I'm gonna snort a line of tzatziki and go.
jokes.setup: Selina's frustration at the book launch
jokes.punchline: Equating Greek sauce with cocaine in an absurd escape fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd metaphor that's both unexpected and character-specific. Economy of language with maximum weird impact.

jokes.text: 'Cause if you ever wanna step several rungs down the ladder, I could always use help from a fellow Daniel.
jokes.setup: Chung offering Dan a job opportunity
jokes.punchline: The 'opportunity' is explicitly described as a step down
jokes.explanation: Solid burn delivered with fake politeness. Shows Chung's passive-aggressive nature.

jokes.text: Yes, but sometimes you have to go down to go up.
jokes.setup: Dan's assertion that he only climbs up ladders
jokes.punchline: Chung's pseudo-profound but meaningless maxim
jokes.explanation: Establishes Chung as a politician who speaks in empty platitudes. Will become a running theme.

jokes.text: But everyone will be watching her, so I guess I can just wing it.
jokes.setup: Chung mentioning he'll be on Face the Nation while Selina is on Meet the Press
jokes.punchline: His fake modesty about being able to wing it since no one will watch
jokes.explanation: Shows Chung's competitive nature masked as humility. Solid character work.

jokes.text: I'd better be going around myself, make sure everyone gets to touch the hem of my garment.
jokes.setup: Chung saying he needs to mingle at his book launch
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Jesus with the hem of garment reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant characterization of Chung's messianic complex. Biblical reference is perfectly pretentious for the character.

jokes.text: I'm gonna steal that, because he who speaks in maxims... can sound wise.
jokes.setup: Amy commenting on Chung's 'go down to go up' saying
jokes.punchline: Creating a maxim about how maxims make you sound wise
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke that becomes its own example. Shows Amy's cynical intelligence about political BS.

jokes.text: This is good. This is like cornbread.
jokes.setup: Amy commenting on the political maneuvering
jokes.punchline: Comparing complex political drama to simple comfort food
jokes.explanation: Amy's detached enjoyment of political chaos. Mundane comparison undercuts the drama.

jokes.text: You looking for some fresh backs to stab?
jokes.setup: Dan and Amy approaching Senator Doyle
jokes.punchline: Doyle immediately assumes they're there to betray someone
jokes.explanation: Establishes Doyle as a cynical insider who knows how Washington works. Direct and cutting.

jokes.text: Why don't you try working an iPad with your dick?
jokes.setup: Doyle suggesting they play a game
jokes.punchline: The 'game' is an impossible and crude physical challenge
jokes.explanation: Absurd and shocking image that perfectly captures Doyle's crude, confrontational personality.

jokes.text: No, he's already got carpal tunnel in that area.
jokes.setup: Doyle's crude iPad challenge to Dan
jokes.punchline: Amy implying Dan masturbates so much he has carpal tunnel
jokes.explanation: Amy's quick wit turning Doyle's insult into an even worse burn on Dan. Perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: Oh, is that what they call it still with two oil guys on it?
jokes.setup: Amy mentioning clean jobs
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the hypocrisy of oil executives on a clean jobs committee
jokes.explanation: Sharp political observation about Washington doublespeak. Shows Doyle's cynical insider knowledge.

jokes.text: Wow, you're gonna have to buy a lot of spray cans to paint that turd gold.
jokes.setup: The clean jobs committee being compromised
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor about trying to make something terrible look good
jokes.explanation: Vivid, memorable metaphor that perfectly captures political spin. Doyle's folksy crudeness works well.

jokes.text: Who uses withdraw as a fuckin' verb besides catholics and butlers?
jokes.setup: Confusion over Doyle saying he's going to 'withdraw'
jokes.punchline: Bizarre categorization of who uses that verb
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected grouping that somehow makes sense. Shows Amy's neurotic overthinking of language.

jokes.text: Maybe the Israeli military every once in a while.
jokes.setup: Amy's question about who uses 'withdraw' as a verb
jokes.punchline: Adding a darkly serious third category to the absurd list
jokes.explanation: Dan's contribution adds dark geopolitical awareness to Amy's absurd observation. Good escalation.

jokes.text: You gotta network to get work, baby. All right? Chung for change.
jokes.setup: Amy wanting to leave the book launch
jokes.punchline: Dan reciting campaign slogans while networking
jokes.explanation: Shows Dan's shameless opportunism and willingness to parrot any politician's slogan for advantage.

jokes.text: You could talk about how you always get a sweaty upper lip underneath studio lights. That shows you're normal.
jokes.setup: Selina needing normal, regular-people talking points
jokes.punchline: Gary's suggestion is embarrassing and unseemly rather than relatable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - trying to be helpful but completely missing the mark. Shows his tone-deafness.

jokes.text: Well, the redskins have a great running game this year and, honestly, that receiving corps over there, Anquan Boldin, not looking good for you.
jokes.setup: Gary playing the interviewer role-playing as a Redskins fan
jokes.punchline: Gary completely butchering the sports knowledge by putting a Ravens player on the Redskins
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary incompetence. His attempt to be helpful by providing sports knowledge backfires spectacularly.

jokes.text: Beer makes me so gassy... about how it always bloats you, beer.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to bet a beer on the game
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately oversharing about digestive issues, Gary encouraging more oversharing
jokes.explanation: Shows both Selina's tendency to overshare and Gary's terrible advice. Builds the cringe factor.

jokes.text: You can talk about that... about how it always bloats you, beer.
jokes.setup: Selina mentioning beer makes her gassy
jokes.punchline: Gary encouraging her to share more digestive TMI publicly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary obliviousness to appropriate public discussion topics.

jokes.text: Did he do this thing where you don't know if you're supposed to hug him or crucify him? Which, by the way, makes me wanna crucify him.
jokes.setup: Asking about Chung's behavior
jokes.punchline: The confusion makes her want to choose the violent option
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from confusion to violence. Shows Selina's short fuse and competitive nature.

jokes.text: Should I fire up the shit eater?
jokes.setup: Selina wanting to shred Chung's book
jokes.punchline: Sue's deadpan nickname for the paper shredder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue line - vulgar but delivered with complete professionalism. Instant classic office nickname.

jokes.text: To vice president Salina Meyer... with an 'a'... from an admiring wannabe.
jokes.setup: Reading Chung's book inscription
jokes.punchline: Chung misspelled Selina's name and called himself an admiring wannabe
jokes.explanation: Double insult - misspelling her name and the backhanded 'wannabe' comment. Sets up major plot point.

jokes.text: What an e-hole.
jokes.setup: The misspelling of Selina's name with an 'a'
jokes.punchline: Amy calling him an asshole but replacing the 'a' with 'e' to match the spelling mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that connects directly to the setup. Amy's quick wit creates a perfect callback pun.

jokes.text: Plus, he doesn't wanna look like he made a massive error picking you... Well, that was lovely.
jokes.setup: Amy explaining why the president won't ditch Selina
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implying Selina was an error, then Selina's dry reaction
jokes.explanation: Amy's foot-in-mouth moment followed by Selina's perfectly timed deadpan response. Good chemistry.

jokes.text: If anything, Chung was too late.
jokes.setup: Discussing Chung's heroic tank rescue story
jokes.punchline: Criticizing a war hero for not being fast enough to prevent burns
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny criticism of genuine heroism. Shows Amy's willingness to attack anything for political advantage.

jokes.text: Do I look like a kindergarten teacher to you, Mike? Actually, yes.
jokes.setup: Sue's rhetorical question about looking like a kindergarten teacher
jokes.punchline: Mike's honest and immediate agreement
jokes.explanation: Mike's blunt honesty cuts through Sue's rhetorical question. Shows his social cluelessness.

jokes.text: You're not even your mom's favorite Jonah, Jonah.
jokes.setup: Jonah calling himself everyone's favorite
jokes.punchline: Implying there are multiple Jonahs and his mom likes the others better
jokes.explanation: Devastating burn with perfect economy. Absurd premise (multiple Jonahs) makes it even more cutting.

jokes.text: Does he want to operate me by remote control? No. That's not technologically possible.
jokes.setup: Selina's sarcastic question about presidential control
jokes.punchline: Jonah's literal response missing the sarcasm entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - completely missing obvious sarcasm with a literalist response. Shows his tone-deafness.

jokes.text: Well, with respect, ma'am, do you think anybody's gonna remember your primary campaign?
jokes.setup: Selina mentioning her immigration stance during primaries
jokes.punchline: Jonah's brutal dismissal of her campaign's memorability
jokes.explanation: Jonah accidentally delivering a crushing truth. His social obliviousness makes the insult even worse.

jokes.text: You know, I meant that more as a slight against the power of recollection of the electorate and not as an attack on the potency of your campaign.
jokes.setup: Jonah realizing his comment was insulting
jokes.punchline: His overcomplicated explanation makes it even worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - trying to fix things with pompous language that makes it worse. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, because you have the social skills of someone who was raised by wolves.
jokes.setup: Selina asking why Jonah didn't phrase things better
jokes.punchline: Comparing his social skills to feral children
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult that perfectly captures Jonah's social ineptitude. Selina's frustration comes through clearly.

jokes.text: God, I love saying that.
jokes.setup: Jonah mentioning he has to get back to the White House
jokes.punchline: His obvious pleasure at name-dropping his workplace
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - his transparent need to impress and name-drop. Shows his insecurity.

jokes.text: My zipper on my Leviathan broke, so I had to drive around trying to find a replacement.
jokes.setup: Asked about her Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact description of a completely bizarre evening
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Sue moment. What's a Leviathan? Why does she need zipper repair on Saturday night? Pure mystery.

jokes.text: You know what I did? I went to bed at 7:00 P.M. 7:00 P.M. on a Saturday night. Even people who are dying of malaria stay up later than that.
jokes.setup: Gary describing his Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself unfavorably to dying malaria patients
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows Gary's pathetic social life. The malaria comparison is unexpectedly specific and funny.

jokes.text: Hearing you say that gives me a massive hard-on. Oh, baby, I'm ready to launch.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she's the vice president
jokes.punchline: Ted's sexual response to her political power
jokes.explanation: Establishes Ted as someone attracted to power. Crude but serves character development purpose.

jokes.text: Why are you so tired? You don't do anything.
jokes.setup: Mike appearing tired
jokes.punchline: Selina's blunt observation about his work ethic
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cutting Selina line. Brutal efficiency in calling out Mike's laziness.

jokes.text: He was born back in China before his dad got his citizenship, so he can't be president or vice president.
jokes.setup: Research on Chung's eligibility
jokes.punchline: Relief at constitutional disqualification (which turns out to be wrong)
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot point. The irony becomes clear when this information proves wrong.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I'm gonna eat him up.
jokes.setup: Learning the interviewer is inexperienced
jokes.punchline: Selina's predatory excitement at facing a rookie
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's competitive nature and confidence. The predator metaphor fits her personality.

jokes.text: A team can't play itself, can it? You can in video games.
jokes.setup: Selina's confusion about sports betting when both people like the same team
jokes.punchline: Gary's helpful but irrelevant video game clarification
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' disconnect from normal sports knowledge. Gary's literalism is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That's sweat. That's lip sweat. / That's shimmer.
jokes.setup: Mike pointing out her sweaty lip on camera
jokes.punchline: Selina's desperate reframing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier setup about sweating being normal

jokes.text: Well, I'll say this... in order to reform, you have to perform.
jokes.setup: Question about filibuster reform
jokes.punchline: Meaningless rhyming political speak
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina falling into empty political clichés when caught off guard. The rhyme makes it more ridiculous.

jokes.text: Hello, penis enlargement clinic.
jokes.setup: Phone ringing during the TV interview
jokes.punchline: Mike's inappropriate phone greeting
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction showing Mike's inappropriate attempt at humor during crisis.

jokes.text: Well, he, uh... Ray Rice, he play nice.
jokes.setup: Trying to respond knowledgeably about Ravens player Ray Rice
jokes.punchline: Falling back on a childish rhyme when she knows nothing
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment. Selina's desperation leads to kindergarten-level rhyming. Painful to watch.

jokes.text: Uh, you know, I would, but I've got a game of solitaire going on, so I think I'll give that a skip.
jokes.setup: Asked if she'll watch Chung on Face the Nation
jokes.punchline: Claiming solitaire is more important than watching her rival
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic dismissal. The pathetic nature of solitaire makes the diss even better.

jokes.text: Well, I'd be surprised because technically he's not an American. I mean, because he wasn't, um, born here, you know, not because he's half Chine...
jokes.setup: Trying to dismiss Chung's presidential potential
jokes.punchline: Accidentally starting to make a racist comment and catching herself
jokes.explanation: Major cringe moment that drives the episode's main crisis. Shows how easily politicians can step in it.

jokes.text: It totally might have just been me, but kind of... didn't it sound a little glitchy in the end or anything like that?
jokes.setup: Gary trying to subtly ask about the recording
jokes.punchline: His completely transparent attempt at being casual
jokes.explanation: Gary's attempt at subtlety is hilariously obvious. Shows his complete lack of social grace under pressure.

jokes.text: All I heard was the sound of a fuckup, okay?
jokes.setup: Gary's awkward attempt to downplay the recording
jokes.punchline: Sound guy's blunt confirmation that he heard everything
jokes.explanation: Direct and cutting response that deflates any hope of covering this up. Professional bluntness.

jokes.text: That's why I'm in here in here, Dan.
jokes.setup: Dan saying it's not out there out there
jokes.punchline: Sue mimicking his phrase structure while asserting her insider knowledge
jokes.explanation: Sue's dry wit playing with the repeated phrase structure. Shows her omniscience about office gossip.

jokes.text: You don't wanna give a loaded gun to that walking silo.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether to tell Jonah about the situation
jokes.punchline: Describing Jonah as a walking silo (tall and empty)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult that captures both Jonah's height and emptiness. Perfect metaphor for his dangerous stupidity.

jokes.text: Well, I'm Sue. It's my job to know everything, Amy.
jokes.setup: Amy asking how Sue knows about the circumstances
jokes.punchline: Sue's matter-of-fact explanation of her omniscience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - omniscient competence delivered deadpan

jokes.text: No, they don't.
jokes.setup: Jonah insisting someone has to make up acronyms
jokes.punchline: Mike's flat contradiction of obviously false logic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan response with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Oh, well, the White House is very much under the moon about that
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to express White House displeasure
jokes.punchline: Mangling the phrase 'over the moon' into something meaningless
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah malapropism. His attempt at sophisticated language backfires predictably.

jokes.text: You can't just make up acronyms, Jonah. Somebody has to, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike objecting to Jonah's made-up acronym
jokes.punchline: Jonah's illogical defense that someone must create acronyms
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's inability to understand basic rules. His logic is technically correct but misses the point.

jokes.text: What do you call that? Bi-casual? Thanks, Mike, but it's Sunday. I work hard, I work fast, and I get hot legs. I say let the whale hang loose.
jokes.setup: Mike mocking Jonah's outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Jonah's bizarre defense involving working hard, hot legs, and whale metaphors
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah weirdness. Multiple bizarre elements that somehow form a coherent character moment.

jokes.text: I work hard, I work fast, and I get hot legs. I say let the whale hang loose.
jokes.setup: Defending his casual Sunday outfit
jokes.punchline: Jonah's bizarre justification mixing work ethic with anatomical metaphors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - inappropriate oversharing with bizarre marine mammal metaphor.

jokes.text: Do you have something that can fit the McClintock shape that's not a Santa suit?
jokes.setup: Asking Dan about appropriate clothing for Mike
jokes.punchline: Describing Mike's body type as requiring special accommodation, excluding Santa suits
jokes.explanation: Great visual insult. The Santa suit exclusion implies Mike is both round and potentially festive-looking.

jokes.text: Converse generally? What about? The weather? / Not about the weather. / It is unseasonably warm, though, okay?
jokes.setup: Mike telling Selina to converse generally
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction followed by Selina doing exactly what he said not to
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing confusion and immediate rule-breaking

jokes.text: Well, it's unseasonably warm.
jokes.setup: Gary specifically told her not to talk about the weather
jokes.punchline: She immediately talks about the weather anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Selina can't help herself. The cringe builds as she does exactly what she was told not to.

jokes.text: if, for some reason, we find out that there was negligence that caused this accident, those who are responsible are gonna have the White House to answer to.
jokes.setup: Selina speaking at the hospital about the accident
jokes.punchline: Making threats about accountability while being completely unaccountable herself
jokes.explanation: Ironic given the episode's events. Shows political grandstanding versus personal responsibility.

jokes.text: 'Cause technically he's not an American. They're taking over our ports. It's like they've invaded us. Come on, this is the White House, not the Yellow House.
jokes.setup: Selina's comment about Chung not being American
jokes.punchline: Crowd escalating to explicitly racist rhetoric
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing how political dog whistles unleash explicit bigotry. Uncomfortable but necessary for the plot.

jokes.text: Gary, that was my fucking flesh that you just... trapped in there.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to help with Selina's belt during the phone call
jokes.punchline: Gary accidentally catching her skin in the belt buckle
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy during serious phone call. Gary's incompetence extends to simple tasks like belt adjustment.

jokes.text: Jennifer, quite frankly, I had more windows in my first apartment than the V.P. currently has in her schedule.
jokes.setup: Amy talking about Selina's schedule availability
jokes.punchline: Comparing schedule openings to apartment windows in a way that suggests both were very few
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that suggests both a crappy first apartment and a very busy schedule. Shows Amy's wit.

jokes.text: Oh, sure! You know what? I blame George Washington. If he hadn't started this whole form of government, then we wouldn't...
jokes.setup: Being blamed for a political problem
jokes.punchline: Escalating blame all the way back to the founding fathers
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of blame that shows Dan's frustration. Taking deflection to its logical extreme.

jokes.text: I'm the vice president of the United States, you stupid little fuckers! These people should be begging me! That door should be half its height so that people can only approach me in my office on their goddamn, motherfucking knees.
jokes.setup: Frustration about having to beg for political support
jokes.punchline: Fantasy about forcing people to crawl to approach her
jokes.explanation: Peak Selina meltdown. The physical door modification fantasy is both absurd and revealing of her character.

jokes.text: You want to break out the white, pointy hoods, too, ma'am?
jokes.setup: Dan suggesting they work with anti-immigration caucus
jokes.punchline: Mike's blunt KKK reference
jokes.explanation: Dark but necessary reality check. Mike occasionally shows moral clarity amid the political maneuvering.

jokes.text: Once you go down that dark country road, the lights go out and the scarecrows come to life.
jokes.setup: Warning against working with extremist politicians
jokes.punchline: Horror movie metaphor for political consequences
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor mixing rural imagery with horror. Mike's occasionally poetic side emerges.

jokes.text: Look, Mike, that's what you always hear because mentally you're in a fuckin' hammock on a back porch.
jokes.setup: Mike's rural/Southern metaphors
jokes.punchline: Dan's accusation that Mike thinks in Southern clichés
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation. Dan notices Mike's tendency toward folksy metaphors and calls it out.

jokes.text: Is McCaulay the one with the nose hair problem like he's got two raccoons in his nostrils?
jokes.setup: Discussing Senator McCaulay
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of his excessive nose hair
jokes.explanation: Grotesque but memorable visual. Shows the staff's attention to embarrassing details about politicians.

jokes.text: What? He's Captain fuckin' America?
jokes.setup: Learning Chung is actually eligible to be president
jokes.punchline: Amy's frustrated realization of how patriotic Chung appears
jokes.explanation: Perfect expression of frustration. The superhero comparison captures how untouchable Chung now seems.

jokes.text: What did Scott use as a research tool, the fuckin' drudge report? That and bathroom walls.
jokes.setup: Scott's research was completely wrong
jokes.punchline: Escalating from bad news sources to even worse ones
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of increasingly unreliable research sources. Dan's addition makes it even more absurd.

jokes.text: I've got a purple heart on my chest, but the one that beats inside of me is red, white, and blue.
jokes.setup: Chung responding to citizenship questions
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top patriotic rhetoric about his heart
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cheesy political rhetoric. Shows Chung's ability to turn crisis into patriotic theater.

jokes.text: I am gonna throw up a leg. And then I'm gonna take that leg and I'm gonna beat Scott to death with it.
jokes.setup: Selina's anger at Scott's incompetence
jokes.punchline: Bizarre threat involving self-dismemberment as a weapon
jokes.explanation: Completely absurd threat that's somehow more threatening than normal violence. Peak Selina creativity in anger.

jokes.text: I've got a purple heart on my chest, but the one that beats inside of me is red, white, and blue.
jokes.setup: Chung defending his patriotism on TV
jokes.punchline: Cheesy patriotic heart metaphor
jokes.explanation: Deliberately cheesy political rhetoric that's cringe-worthy by design

jokes.text: Chief? Shouldn't that be chiefess?
jokes.setup: Amy introduced as chief of staff
jokes.punchline: O'Brien's sexist 'correction' of her title
jokes.explanation: Establishes O'Brien's casual sexism. Sets the tone for how awful he'll be throughout the scene.

jokes.text: I'd like to know if you're going to offend us with some chickenshit deal that means we have to leave before the food comes.
jokes.setup: O'Brien agreeing to meet
jokes.punchline: His main concern is whether he'll get to eat
jokes.explanation: Shows O'Brien's priorities and crude nature. Politics is secondary to his dinner plans.

jokes.text: Now, if you had bad neighbors who came into your yard every night to crap a buttful of drugs and beans on your flowers, you might think about building a fence.
jokes.setup: O'Brien explaining his anti-immigration stance
jokes.punchline: Grotesque metaphor involving defecation, drugs, and beans
jokes.explanation: Deliberately crude and racist metaphor. Shows the level of discourse they're dealing with.

jokes.text: Yeah, but ironically, the only affordable option would be immigrant labor.
jokes.setup: Discussion of building a border fence
jokes.punchline: Pointing out they'd need the very people they're trying to keep out to build it
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about the irony of anti-immigration policy. Dan shows his intelligence occasionally.

jokes.text: Now you keep talking like that, sweetheart, and we'll be having cigars at the end of this dinner. Just not Cuban ones.
jokes.setup: Amy agreeing with his anti-immigration stance
jokes.punchline: O'Brien's patronizing approval and random dig at Cuba
jokes.explanation: Shows O'Brien's sexist condescension and reflexive anti-Communist stance. Character consistency.

jokes.text: You know what... I'm coming over there, like, right away. Uh, you know, when you come over, can you try and sneak in incognito? I got Korean neighbors and I don't think they'd like the idea of me dating you.
jokes.setup: Selina arranging to see Ted
jokes.punchline: Ted's racist assumption about his Korean neighbors' reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows Ted's casual racism after Selina's earlier gaffe. Connects the personal and political themes.

jokes.text: It's about seven inches, hangs between my legs... The thing. I like to call him sergeant Ted.
jokes.setup: Ted describing something sexual
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking about his phone (probably)
jokes.explanation: Basic misdirection humor, more crude than clever

jokes.text: Is that some kind of a joke or something? It's not funny at all. Fuck off, Ted.
jokes.setup: Ted's racist comment about his Korean neighbors
jokes.punchline: Selina's outraged reaction to Ted's racism while missing her own hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy moment - Selina condemns Ted's racism while ignoring her own earlier slip.

jokes.text: What happened to your hot pants? Oh, the hot pants picked up some negative traction. I realize now they're unbecoming of my office.
jokes.setup: Jonah's earlier outfit choice
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-professional explanation for changing clothes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier fashion choice. Shows Jonah trying to sound professional and failing.

jokes.text: I will appear to love filibuster reform as much as I appear to love my idiot son. And for my wife's sake, I appear to love that wretched shitnia lot.
jokes.setup: O'Brien promising support for filibuster reform
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his fake love for his son and using 'shitnia' as an intensifier
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction revealed casually. The made-up word 'shitnia' is perfectly crude for O'Brien.

jokes.text: Sometimes you've gotta go down to go up.
jokes.setup: Amy reflecting on the dark compromise they just made
jokes.punchline: Using Chung's earlier meaningless platitude ironically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that now has dark meaning. Amy's found the perfect context for Chung's empty wisdom.

jokes.text: Something about 'getting fucked every which way.' Direct quote.
jokes.setup: Asked where the VP is
jokes.punchline: Sue's matter-of-fact quotation of Selina's crude departure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue delivery - professional reporting of unprofessional language. Her 'direct quote' disclaimer is great.

jokes.text: How many times can she be underneath that? She's got such a little body and he seemed like a big man.
jokes.setup: Overhearing Selina's sexual conversation
jokes.punchline: Gary's inappropriate analysis of their physical compatibility
jokes.explanation: Peak Gary inappropriateness. His clinical approach to his boss's sex life is deeply uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Dan, you have about 10 seconds to stop distracting me, or the next thing that they'll find at the back of that couch are your remains.
jokes.setup: Dan apparently bothering Sue while she works
jokes.punchline: Sue's matter-of-fact death threat with specific body disposal location
jokes.explanation: Classic Sue threat - specific, professional, and terrifying. The couch detail makes it more concrete and funny.

tmdb_overview: Dan and Amy try to figure out if an Asian-American war hero-cum-governor could be a political threat to Selina. Meanwhile, Selina and Mike prep for her "Meet the Press" interview; and Gary and Amy make a startling discovery about Selina's romantic life.
insight: Political incompetence hits 88 jokes in 22 minutes—Veep's densest episode yet.
